content.problemStatement:
Chef is experimenting in his kitchen. He has NNN spices (numbered 000 through N−1N-1N−1) with types S0,S1,…,SN−1S_0, S_1, \ldots, S_{N-1}S0​,S1​,…,SN−1​.

You should answer QQQ queries. Each query is described by two integers LLL and RRR. In each query, Chef wants to use the spices SL,SL+1,…,SRS_L, S_{L+1}, \ldots, S_RSL​,SL+1​,…,SR​ in some order to make a dish. However, he has no clue about the order in which he should use them to make a perfect dish, so he wants to try all possible orders (sequences created by permuting these R−L+1R-L+1R−L+1 spices) to find out. Tell Chef the number of distinct orders (sequences) of spices he needs to try. Since this number can be very large, compute it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

Two sequences of spices are considered different if there is a valid index iii such that the type of the iii-th spice in one sequence is different from the type of the iii-th spice used in the other sequence.

Note that the input is encoded so that you have to answer the queries online.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers S0,S1,S2,…,SN−1S_0, S_1, S_2, \ldots, S_{N-1}S0​,S1​,S2​,…,SN−1​.
Each of the next QQQ lines contains four space-separated integers L1L_1L1​, L2L_2L2​, R1R_1R1​ and R2R_2R2​ describing a query. The parameters LLL and RRR can be calculated in the following way:

Let's denote the answer to the previous query by aaa; for the first query, a=0a = 0a=0.
Calculate L=(L1⋅a+L2)%NL = (L_1 \cdot a + L_2) \% NL=(L1​⋅a+L2​)%N and R=(R1⋅a+R2)%NR = (R_1 \cdot a + R_2) \% NR=(R1​⋅a+R2​)%N.
Then, if L>RL \gt RL>R, swap LLL and RRR.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer — the number of distinct orders of spices modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
